title: D-16 — Audit immutability + verify hardening: (1) a **DB-permission grant** that makes the `audit` schema INSERT/SELECT-only (block UPDATE/DELETE for out-of-app / DBA writes), and (2) a **nightly Hangfire chain-verify job** (C-INS-02) that runs `GET /api/audit/verify` on a schedule and alerts on a broken link.

D-17 — Enriched before/after for **owned-child** audit rows. Child ops (e.g. Research add/verify Finding, add/set-status Recommendation) emit an `AuditEvent` (INV-005 holds) but with **empty before/after**: `AuditCaptureInterceptor` captures only mutated `AuditableEntity` rows, and owned children (`Finding`/`Recommendation`) are `BaseEntity`, while the parent aggregate's scalars are unchanged on a child add. Enhancement = make owned children `AuditableEntity` (audit columns → migration) + emit the child-op audit with the **child** as subject (`FND-`/`REC-`) so the capture correlates.

D-18 — Audit-append **concurrency contention.** `SqlAuditSink` takes no application-level lock: two genuinely concurrent state-changing requests both read the same chain tip (`TipHashAsync`) and race the `audit.AuditEvents` `PreviousHash` UNIQUE index — the loser gets SQL error 2601 (duplicate key) and the whole operation **500s** (fail-closed, but a hard error rather than a retry). Accepted at design time (ADR-0009/0026 + the `SqlAuditSink.cs` `ponytail:` note — "negligible at ≤20-user on-prem scale; if it ever bites, serialize via `sp_getapplock` or retry"). **Reproduced 2026-07-13** by the P15b live VR: the SPA's login-time `Membership.ProfileSynced` append raced an immediate seed burst, so one `Research.MissionProposed` lost the race and 500'd (audit seq 12/13 both chained off tip `1f738021`). Fix (the pre-named upgrade path): a **bounded retry** on 2601/2627 inside `AppendAsync` — re-read tip, recompute hash, re-insert — which preserves chain linearity + the fail-closed guarantee; or `sp_getapplock` serialization. **2026-07-13 — operator chose to fix now**, then (after adversarial review) to fix it **robustly** rather than cheaply. Arc: a first pass added a bounded optimistic-retry in `SqlAuditSink`; CI then showed the 8-way stress case **deadlocks (SQL 1205)**, whose victim's transaction is rolled back → in-place retry cannot recover. A devil's-advocate review further found a **sink-level app lock is lock-order-unsafe** (handlers like `IssueDecision`/`VerifyAction` emit-then-write-more, inverting `{lock, module-rows}` order). **✅ RESOLVED 2026-07-13 via ADR-0028 (serialize at tx-open):** `AmbientTransaction.EnsureStartedAsync` takes a **transaction-scoped exclusive `sp_getapplock`** ('acmp-audit-chain') immediately after `BeginTransaction` — before any module write — so every audited write-command acquires the chain lock first (one consistent order → no cross-handler deadlock) and appends serialize (no fork, no 1205). Held to commit ⇒ next command reads a committed tip; `THROW` on lock-timeout ⇒ fail-closed; SQL-Server-only (degrades to no-op elsewhere). The **denial/autocommit path** (no ambient tx) keeps the bounded retry as its backstop (broadened to catch the `PreviousHash` **or** `Hash` index race; deadlock-free — no crosswise module locks). Tests: `Concurrent_commands_all_commit_without_forking_the_audit_chain` (8 write-commands serialize) + `Concurrent_denials_all_record_their_audit_via_the_retry_without_forking_the_chain` (8 denials converge via retry) + the same-tx atomicity suite, all green (CI-proven for the write-command deadlock case). No schema/migration.

D-19 — **Flaky frontend test — `src/Acmp.Web/src/features/decisions/DecisionPage.test.tsx:174`.** Intermittently reads `/decisions/DECN-2026-008` where `/decisions/DECN-2026-015` is expected; unrelated red on the D-18 PR #111 CI run 2026-07-13, green on the immediate re-run. **⚠ The original diagnosis recorded here was wrong** and is corrected in place (P17a, 2026-07-17): it blamed "non-deterministic decision selection (order-/hash-dependent pick over the mocked decisions list)" — **there is no decisions list and no selection in that test.** `useDecision` is mocked to one fixed object (`:54-56`) and the supersede mutation is a fixed `mockResolvedValue({ key: 'DECN-2026-015' })` (`:78`); both are deterministic. **Real mechanism:** `DECN-2026-008` is simply `setup()`'s initial route (`:62`), and `DECN-2026-015` is reachable only *after* `SupersedeDialog.onConfirm` awaits `mutateAsync` and then calls `navigate()` (`SupersedeDialog.tsx:70-81`). The assertion read `expect(await screen.findByTestId('loc')).toHaveTextContent(...)` — but `loc` (`LocationDisplay`) is **always mounted**, so `findByTestId` resolved on its first poll without waiting for anything, and `toHaveTextContent` then evaluated **exactly once** against a pathname the post-await `navigate()` may not yet have flushed. Hence the initial route, and hence why a re-run passes. **Scope note:** the sibling sync assertions at `:168`/`:188`/`:209`/`:217-220` are **not** affected — `mutateAsync` is *invoked* synchronously inside `onConfirm`, so an awaited `user.click` guarantees the call happened; only code after the handler's own `await` races. They were deliberately left untouched.
activation_trigger: ✅ Done 2026-07-17 (P17a) — retry the assertion rather than the element: await waitFor(() => expect(screen.getByTestId('loc')).toHaveTextContent('/decisions/DECN-2026-015')). :235 left as-is (sync getByTestId asserting the path stays on the error path — correct there, and preceded by a real findByText await).

title: D-15 — **⭐ HIGH PRIORITY — Topic *Prepare* (Accepted→Prepared) has no UI affordance.** `POST /api/topics/{id}/prepare` (BL-036 / AC-035) shipped **backend-only**: the SPA never calls it (only `accept`/`return` are wired, in the kanban). So no topic reaches `Prepared`, the agenda-builder pool (`GET /topics?status=Prepared`) is **permanently empty in-product**, and "Search topics" shows nothing — the intake→agenda **core loop is broken for real users**. Discovered 2026-07-07 from an operator bug report; root-caused against `docs/domain/entity-lifecycles.md §1` (W4) + the design files (which omit Prepare entirely — the kanban has 5 buckets, no `Prepared`; the timeline jumps Accepted→Scheduled).
activation_trigger: Activate immediately. Fix (frontend-only, no ADR): usePrepareTopic + a "Mark prepared" button on an Accepted topic detail (TopicDetail.tsx .dt-actions); gating = show-and-enforce (render for any Accepted topic, backend 403s — the owner is often a plain Member, so a role gate would hide it from the right person); invalidate three query keys (backlog + ['topics','prepared'] + ['topics','detail',key]); a "Prepared" badge on kanban cards (Accepted & Prepared share the accepted bucket, topicMeta.ts, so Prepared is otherwise invisible); reword the agenda-pool empty-state to point to the action. Optional W4-completeness: notify the Secretary on prepare (PrepareTopicHandler only audits today; the INotificationChannel seam exists). Open sub-questions (raise an OQ if pursued): the domain has no "un-prepare" / defer-from-Prepared transition (a mis-prepared topic is stuck until scheduled); and design-update-owed (the design omits Prepare). ✅ RESOLVED 2026-07-09 (Tier 3): "Mark prepared" button on the Accepted-topic detail (usePrepareTopic, show-and-enforce, inline 403) + Prepared kanban badge + reworded agenda empty-state + PrepareTopicHandler Secretary notification (skip-self); core-loop.spec now clicks the button instead of a direct-HTTP prepare (the swapped spec was run locally against the live stack — green; CI re-validates on the PR), and TopicApiTests proves the new DI seams. Un-prepare gap → OQ-049 (default = defer). Design-update-owed still stands.

title: D-21 — **Container hardening — 3 HIGH Dockerfile misconfigs (Trivy).** P16b's Trivy fs scan (gating on CRITICAL) surfaced 3 HIGH findings, non-blocking under the CRITICAL gate and no longer shown in CI once `--severity CRITICAL` filters the report: **DS-0002** (no non-root `USER`) on `deploy/Dockerfile.backend` **and** `deploy/Dockerfile.web`, and **DS-0029** (`apt-get install` missing `--no-install-recommends`) on `deploy/Dockerfile.sqlserver:8-14`. 0 CRITICAL, 0 vuln, 0 secret.
activation_trigger: B4 kickoff — add non-root USER + --no-install-recommends, then tighten the Trivy gate to --severity CRITICAL,HIGH

title: D-22 — **Inline `style={{}}` usage in the SPA — style hygiene, NOT a security control.** ~110 inline-style sites across ~25 components (18 static, 7 dynamic + `SortableList.tsx`'s @dnd-kit drag transform). P16-B3 planned to refactor them all (static → CSS classes, dynamic → a `useCssVars` CSSOM hook, plus a @dnd-kit CSSOM shim) in order to drop `style-src 'unsafe-inline'`. **Verified 2026-07-16 that the refactor is unnecessary for that goal:** CSP governs `<style>` elements and `style=""` attributes, not the CSSOM, and react-dom applies the `style` prop via CSSOM writes (`style.setProperty(...)` / `style[name] = v` — confirmed in the react-dom source), never serialising a style attribute. With no SSR (client-rendered Vite SPA), no inline style in `index.html`, DOMPurify's allowlist excluding `style` attrs + `<style>` tags, and no CSS-in-JS dependency, `style-src 'self'` ships **with the code as-is** (browser-verified: CSSOM writes apply, `setAttribute('style')` and injected `<style>` are blocked). C-WEB-02 is therefore **Met without this work**.
activation_trigger: A frontend consistency/design-system pass elects to take it on. Do not activate for CSP reasons — that rationale is disproven.

title: D-20 — **Confidentiality ABAC (C-AUTHZ-04) — `Restricted` topics.** `security-controls.md` C-AUTHZ-04 specifies a `ConfidentialityRequirement` ABAC handler restricting `Restricted` topics (security findings, sensitive partner matters) to Chair/Coord + explicit grantees + Owner/Assignees + Auditor, excluded from default stream visibility **and search results**. Verified 2026-07-16 (P16a): there is **no `Confidentiality`/`Restricted`/`Sensitivity` field anywhere in `src/**/*.cs`** — so this is a **feature**, not a hardening pass (domain field + migration + create/edit UI + search-exclusion + ABAC handler + tests), out of P16 scope.
activation_trigger: Dedicated Confidentiality-ABAC feature slice (raise an FR/OQ)

title: D-23 — **P19 closeable-gap follow-up (four buildable items).** P19 was scoped reports-only, so the buildable gaps its audit surfaced are collected here rather than built in the audit slice. (a) **AC-043/FR-034** keyboard priority reorder in the backlog — full-stack, mirror the AgendaBuilder AC-044 ±1-delta pattern (new `POST /api/topics/{id}/priority/move {delta}` neighbor-swap + kanban move-up/down buttons); closes A-03's backlog leg. (b) **AC-032** reject→submitter notify (inject `INotificationChannel` into `RejectTopicHandler`) + **AC-057** Secretary SLA-breach Hangfire sweep mirroring `SweepActionRemindersHandler` (+ a persisted `SlaNotifiedAt` marker on `Topic` + migration). (c) **BL-016** localized validation via an error-code contract translated by the client i18n (AC-049 the real gap; AC-030 already client-localized). (d) **wcag22aa** e2e axe-tag bump on `rtl-a11y.spec.ts` + extend the sweep to the kanban view. Also fold in the **F-04 de-flake** of the `SqlAuditSink` tip-race coverage. Each flips its AC only after its live-leg lands.
